hq: New Brunswick, New Jersey, United States
aging_relevance_reasoning: Johnson & Johnson (NYSE: JNJ) is a diversified pharma company (Janssen/Innovative Medicine) with major marketed franchises in immunology/inflammation and cardiometabolic disease—two areas that directly intersect aging biology (e.g., chronic inflammation and deregulated nutrient sensing hallmarks). However, in the public high-level materials reviewed here, the company does not prominently position a dedicated 'aging/longevity' R&D program as a core strategy. Sources for corporate identification, headquarters, and therapeutic focus areas: https://www.nyse.com/quote/XNYS:JNJ ; https://www.jnj.com/contact-us ; https://www.janssen.com/therapeutic-areas

existing_longevity_assets.indication: Type 2 diabetes mellitus (and related risk reduction claims per label; see FDA-approved labeling)
existing_longevity_assets.relevance: Metabolic intervention relevant to aging biology via modulation of glucose handling and nutrient-sensing-associated pathways; clinically used in cardiometabolic disease populations that overlap with aging-related morbidity.

existing_longevity_assets.indication: Plaque psoriasis, psoriatic arthritis, Crohn's disease, ulcerative colitis (see FDA-approved labeling)
existing_longevity_assets.relevance: Anti-inflammatory biologic (IL-12/23) relevant to the chronic inflammation hallmark; may indirectly intersect longevity biology by modulating systemic inflammatory signaling in immune-mediated disease.

existing_longevity_assets.indication: Plaque psoriasis and psoriatic arthritis (see FDA-approved labeling)
existing_longevity_assets.relevance: Anti-inflammatory biologic (IL-23) relevant to aging-associated chronic inflammation and altered intercellular communication (cytokine signaling), though not developed as a longevity therapy.

Autologous CAR-T cell therapy demonstrates advanced cell therapy capabilities that are potentially relevant to future immune-rejuvenation approaches, although the approved indication is oncology rather than aging.

recent_acquisitions.relevance: Medtech/cardiovascular acquisition that expands capabilities in circulatory support; cardiovascular health is tightly linked to aging-related morbidity, but this is not a direct longevity/aging R&D acquisition.

Oncology-focused acquisition for antibody-drug conjugate (ADC) platform/asset(s); oncology platforms can intersect senescence biology, but the stated rationale is cancer-focused rather than aging-focused.

Cardiovascular device acquisition (intravascular lithotripsy) aligned to atherosclerotic disease treatment; relevant to aging-associated cardiovascular risk but not positioned as a longevity R&D effort.

acquisition_pattern.preferred_stage: Primarily commercial/late-stage assets and established platforms (in both medtech and biopharma), based on cited acquisitions in this profile (analysis based on sources listed).
acquisition_pattern.recent_trend: Continued willingness to deploy large capital for cardiovascular medtech plus targeted biopharma platform expansion in oncology (analysis based on sources listed).
